title: englerina subquadrangularis
intro: Englerina subquadrangularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae, commonly known as a mistletoe species. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as treating fever, digestive issues, and skin infections, due to its bioactive compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids. Herbal preparations of Englerina subquadrangularis are typically made by infusing the leaves or stems in water or oil to create a medicinal tea or tincture.
